level_signals: Track record building AI/ML-powered products at an early-to-mid stage
Sole PM owning a major product surface end to end

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience, with a track record building AI/ML-powered products at an early-to-mid stage.
You've worked on AI applications: written evals, reasoned about model behavior, picked tradeoffs between agentic architectures.
You'll spend significant time with lawyers and become a quasi-domain-expert on legal contracts and review workflows.
You thrive in ambiguity, move with urgency, and have proven success in early-stage startup environments.
You can engage with engineering on architecture, agent design, and eval infrastructure. You don't need to write code.
You ship and iterate; you don't over-plan.
You take full responsibility for outcomes, not just deliverables.
Hands-on experience building AI/ML-powered products in production
Experience working with domain experts to shape an AI product (legal, medical, financial, scientific)
Background that includes seeing a product evolve from early stage to scale

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Personal time spent in the AI sauce is a strong plus.

scope.surface: Crosby's AI redlining system — product used by internal lawyers to review every contract; 0→1 build then scale across all contract types
scope.business_model: AI-native law firm, B2B legal services with proprietary tooling
core_responsibilities: Own the vision, product and roadmap end to end for the redlining product
Take the product surface from 0 → 1, then scale it across all contract types to unlock full automation
Design and operate the evaluation infrastructure for the redlining product
Spend significant time with internal legal team and design partners to surface requirements
Translate quality, automation, and customer outcomes into measurable signals
Move quickly under ambiguity; ship, learn, iterate

green_flags: Clear and specific product ownership — AI redlining system for a defined use case
Named clients (Cursor, Ramp, Cognition) signal real traction and quality bar
Founders have strong pedigree (Stanford Law, first 15 engineers at Ramp)
JD is unusually concrete about the product surface and responsibilities
NY Labor Law disclosure signals pay range transparency compliance (even if not listed in scraped content)
red_flags: Compensation range referenced in JD text as required but not actually listed in the scraped content
Full-time in-person in New York City — eliminates remote or hybrid candidates entirely
Very early-stage company with significant ambiguity; 0→1 role requires high tolerance for uncertainty
Work authorization and visa sponsorship policy not stated

location.type (inferred from 'We work together, in person, in New York City' — not a formal policy statement)

The bullets under 'Who you are' mix unconditional requirements with implied nice-to-haves without clear demarcation; 'Hands-on experience building AI/ML-powered products in production', 'Experience working with domain experts', and 'Background that includes seeing a product evolve from early stage to scale' appear as standalone bullets without a header — treated as hard requirements given they are unconditional statements.
